title: rivina laevis f. humilis
intro: Rivina laevis f. humilis, also known as the "Sensitive Plant" or "Rivina", is a flowering plant species in the Phytolaccaceae family, native to the Americas and the Caribbean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rivina laevis f. humilis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make infusions, teas, or tinctures, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment.
